items.title: 🎚️CodexBar 0.21 Abacus AI provider, Codex Pro $100 support, safer OpenAI web extras, fixed loc…
items.content: 🎚️CodexBar 0.21 Abacus AI provider, Codex Pro $100 support, safer OpenAI web extras, fixed local cost scanning, z. ai 5h quotas, Antigravity/Cursor/Ollama fixes, faster refreshes, macOS 26 icon fix and more. The big issue with too much CPU usage was an OpenAI web fetch and is now disabled for new installs. Also keychain issues are resolved. Shoutout to @RatulSarna for maintaining. https://t.co/GSzgKVfBLL

items.title: That was the case in December. 4 months and thousands of work hours later, we have a great secu…
items.content: That was the case in December. 4 months and thousands of work hours later, we have a great security concept; you can go all yolo, use a sandbox (Docker or OpenShell), there are allow-lists and per-access exec allow/deny prompts. There’s hundreds of security researchers that pen-tested it.

items.title: If you look at GPT 5.4-Cyber and it's ability for closed source reverse engineering, I have bad…
items.content: If you look at GPT 5.4-Cyber and it's ability for closed source reverse engineering, I have bad news for you. I do very much feel the pain though, there's hundreds of teams that try to poke holes into @openclaw. Our response has been of rapid iteration and code hardening. Which did introduce occasiaonal regression (and yes you all been yelling at me), but I see as the only way forward. I would be very careful of other open source projects/harnesses that ignore this work and do not publish their…

items.title: Harnesses can now been plugins - meaning it'd be trivial to replace the default Pi harness with…
items.content: Harnesses can now been plugins - meaning it'd be trivial to replace the default Pi harness with e.g. Anthropic’s SDK or another custom one. This won't magically allow Claude subscription, but it's useful to compare different implementations. Using codex as harness will likely weaken personality, but should increase agentic mode to keep the agent working on longer-horizon tasks. So far I gated strict mode to GPT since it's not yet tested with other models, but it's trivial to modify that if you…
items.summary: Harnesses can now been plugins - meaning it'd be trivial to replace the default Pi harness with e.g. Anthropic’s SDK or another custom one. This won't magically allow Claude subscription, but it's useful to compare diff…

items.title: 2) Native Codex as the OpenClaw harness: plugins.entries.codex.enabled = true agents.defaults.m…
items.content: 2) Native Codex as the OpenClaw harness: plugins.entries.codex.enabled = true agents.defaults.model = "codex/gpt-5.4" agents.defaults.embeddedHarness = { runtime: "codex", fallback: "none" } Codex owns threads, resume, compaction, app-server execution. https://t.co/9DDpY6TMqf
items.summary: 2) Native Codex as the OpenClaw harness: plugins.entries.codex.enabled = true agents.defaults.model = "codex/gpt-5.4" agents.defaults.embeddedHarness = { runtime: "codex", fallback: "none" } Codex owns threads, resume,…

items.title: Two experiments in the next @openclaw to address some "GPT is lazy" issues: 1) Strict mode: age…
items.content: Two experiments in the next @openclaw to address some "GPT is lazy" issues: 1) Strict mode: agents.defaults.embeddedPi.executionContract = "strict-agentic" This tells GPT-5.x to keep working: read more code, call tools, make changes, or return a real blocker instead of stopping at “here’s the plan.” https://t.co/QMdeus6Y5J
